expectations.text: Output contains all 4 rounds (Round 1 through Round 4)
expectations.evidence: Round 1: Visionary Proposes, Round 2: Skeptic Challenges, Round 3: Visionary Defends, Round 4: Pragmatist Decides — all present

expectations.text: Visionary, Skeptic, and Pragmatist personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: Visionary proposes bold B2C expansion, Skeptic raises 7 challenges with severity ratings, Pragmatist delivers modify-and-proceed verdict

expectations.text: Round 1 includes a thesis, rationale with 3-5 arguments, timeline, resources needed, and upside scenario
expectations.evidence: Thesis present, 5 rationale arguments, detailed monthly timeline, specific resource needs ($150K, 5 people), quantified upside scenario

expectations.text: Round 2 contains numbered challenges with severity ratings (critical/significant/minor)
expectations.evidence: 7 numbered challenges: 3 critical, 3 significant, 1 minor — all labeled with severity

expectations.text: Round 3 addresses each numbered challenge from Round 2, with concessions or counters
expectations.evidence: All 7 challenges addressed: 5 conceded with mitigations, 2 countered with evidence

expectations.text: Round 4 contains a verdict that is one of: proceed, modify-and-proceed, defer, reject
Round 4 includes numbered action items with who/what/when
expectations.evidence: 7 numbered action items with specific owners (VP Product, CTO, CFO, CEO), deliverables, and dates

expectations.text: Debate metrics table with challengesRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesDefeated, challengesConceded, confidenceScore
expectations.evidence: Metrics table present: challengesRaised=7, challengesSurvived=2, challengesDefeated=0, challengesConceded=5, confidenceScore=5

expectations.text: Round 4 includes a risks accepted section referencing specific challenges
expectations.evidence: 4 risks accepted with specific references to team diversion, waitlist conversion, consumer UX, and runway pressure

expectations.text: Round 4 includes a review date for revisiting the decision
